name: Cytochrome P450 family 2 subfamily D member 6
publicSummary: CYP2D6 is modeled as a systemic functional-null pilot: inherited no-function, decreased function, poor-metabolizer labels, induced phenoconversion, tissue context, substrates, exposure biomarkers, and boundaries stay separate.
oneLine: A systemic enzyme-function case that demonstrates inherited null alleles, whole-gene deletion, reduced function, hybrid alleles, and drug-induced functional nulls.
whyStrong: CYP2D6 is a high-value null model because an inherited true null can mean the body lacks a CYP2D6 enzyme function across expression compartments, not only in liver medication metabolism. Strong inhibitors can create partial functional nulls, but they may not reproduce the same whole-body state.
globalBoundaries: Educational research reference only; not diagnostic interpretation and not medication advice.
CYP2D6 true-null interpretation requires copy-number, deletion, hybrid-allele, and phasing-aware calling.
Poor metabolizer is a clinical translation label, not automatically a whole-body true-null state.
Endogenous and tissue-local routes are pathway-capacity questions unless direct human null phenotype evidence exists.
confidenceSummary: Strong human drug-response and enzyme-function evidence; unusually important CNS layer with human brain expression/function evidence plus endogenous-substrate biochemical and animal-brain evidence.
